bull.b: A multi-year cash runway materially reduces near-term financing pressure and gives management time to execute multiple mid-stage readouts and regulatory interactions. This durable liquidity supports clinical advancement and strategic optionality without immediate dilution if milestones proceed as planned.

Robust, dose-dependent restoration of functional M‑AAT and large reductions in toxic Z‑AAT with sustained editing and a favorable safety profile indicate a differentiated therapeutic profile. This durable biomarker and safety signal supports potential regulatory pathways and a lasting position in AATD treatment if conf

A durable body‑composition signal that preserves muscle and reduces visceral fat supports a clear clinical differentiation versus existing weight-loss agents. Acceptance of the Phase IIa multidose portion and plans for combination/maintenance studies create a lasting strategic pathway for positioning WVE‑007 in obesity

bear.b: Sustained negative operating and free cash flow at this magnitude creates ongoing funding dependence beyond milestone variability. Even with a multi-year runway, persistent high burn increases likelihood of future equity raises or partnerships, which can dilute shareholders and complicate long-term planning if program

Deep, persistent operating losses mean current revenues do not cover the cost base and the company lacks profitability leverage. This structural unprofitability constrains reinvestment capacity and raises execution risk for commercial-scale activities unless cost structures shift or recurrent product revenue emerges.

Pending regulator decisions on endpoints and accelerated pathways create material execution risk; ambiguous regulatory expectations can force larger or altered trials.
